prompt:
Our ops rotation hands off between shifts with a tiny click CLI, shiftlog.py, and it has two behaviors that are driving me up the wall.

First: the tags column will not go away. The handoff wrapper renders the log twice — once with `--show-tags` for the wiki, then plain for the terminal summary — and the plain render comes out WITH a tags column anyway. A colleague then ran `--show-tags` in a loop and the header actually grew: id | shift | message | tags | tags | tags. Run each command in its own fresh shell and everything looks perfectly fine, which is why nobody caught this in manual testing — but the wrapper (and the test suite) drives the CLI in-process, and there it goes wrong every single time.

Second: `shiftlog show <id>` with an id that doesn't exist prints the error line like it should, but the CI guard step stays green — `echo $?` says 0. It must exit 1 so a bad handoff reference actually fails the pipeline.

This runner box is fresh, nothing installed: set up the project venv from the pinned requirements.txt the standard way (.venv in the repo), then reproduce with `bash run_verify.sh` — that's the exact suite CI runs (test_shiftlog.py). Fix shiftlog.py; the tests are right, don't touch them.
